title: [feature request] async
body:
hello!

this lib is synchronous for now

can async support be added?

comments.body: hey @gmankab, that's a big change to turn the library async (besides, it would probably pull more dependencies in). what's the use case to use async?

in my use case i want parallel use of different podman containers

i decided to make async requests to podman socket with httpx

I see, I believe the best here would be to have a wrapper around podmany-py that targets your specific use case.

Also, I think that generally the benefit of having podman-py methods be async is strictly limited to a couple of functions, and the rest will fall into race conditions, or async exceptions that will be difficult to handle or to maintain.

I'll close the issue for now, but I would consider it reopening it for a stronger case or for a proof of concept that us, maintainers, don't have the capacity to provide right now :)
